# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: logging-agent-deployment
  labels:
    app: logging-agent
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: logging-agent
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: logging-agent
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: logging-agent
        image: your-logging-agent-image:latest # Replace with your actual logging agent image (e.g., fluentd, filebeat)
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        # Add your agent's configuration, command, and arguments here
        # For example, if it's a filebeat agent:
        # command: ["filebeat", "-e"]
        # args: ["-c", "/etc/filebeat/filebeat.yml"]
        volumeMounts:
        - name: varlog
          mountPath: /var/log
          readOnly: true # Usually logs are read-only
        - name: vardockerlib
          mountPath: /var/lib/docker
          readOnly: true # Usually used for metadata, not writing
        # If your agent needs access to the root filesystem for other logs
        # - name: rootfs
        #   mountPath: /host
        #   readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: varlog
        hostPath:
          path: /var/log
          type: DirectoryOrCreate
      - name: vardockerlib
        hostPath:
          path: /var/lib/docker
          type: DirectoryOrCreate
      # - name: rootfs # Example for mounting the entire root filesystem
      #   hostPath:
      #     path: /
      #     type: DirectoryOrCreate
      # nodeSelector: # Optional: Target specific nodes if needed
      #   kubernetes.io/os: linux
      # serviceAccountName: your-logging-agent-service-account # Optional: If your agent needs specific RBAC permissions
      # tolerations: # Optional: If running on master nodes or nodes with specific taints
      # - key: "node-role.kubernetes.io/master"
      #   operator: "Exists"
      #   effect: "NoSchedule"
      # - key: "CriticalAddonsOnly"
      #   operator: "Exists"
      #   effect: "NoExecute"
      # securityContext: # Optional: To run with elevated privileges if required by the agent
      #   runAsUser: 0 # Run as root
      #   runAsGroup: 0
      #   fsGroup: 0
